entry.title: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
The 👉 SAP Community Escape House has a new addition!!

We've built a garage for our workshop to build all kinds of stuff using SAP Build. Use your wits and knowledge about SAP Build – specifically SAP Build Process Automation – to try to escape.

WARNING: It will be really tough to escape – you have been warned! So I will be providing tips throughout the month to help you through, so subscribe to this post.

For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges created by the Developer Advocates will happen in the SAP Community Escape House (shout out to @nicoschoenteich for his amazing creativity). Each month we will unlock a new room in the house with a specific focus. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding – or no-code 😉 – tasks/questions. This month is all about one of the tools inside SAP Build.

Here are our previous challenges:

  • January: UI5
  • February: Integration Suite
  • March: AI

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID.

If you don't know your user ID, click Need Help? This will display a link to your community settings page.

Then you will see your ID here:

Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the SAP Build garage.

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips.

Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and, eventually, instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room.

The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community – and the unending esteem of your fellow SAP developers, business users and implementers. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

Good luck!

😺

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
@Sabarim_07 If there's something specific you are having trouble with, let me know. The Mass Maintenance of Service Orders would require a destination to a DMS service and a destination to an S/4HANA system using a user with permission to the Service Orders OData service.

I have been able to get it work -- it is actually pretty cool, using processes, automations, DMS, S/4HANA, Web Call and script activities and more. I will write a blog or do a video on this sometime.

Let me know if I can help you in any way.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
@vishalakshmi You do not have to install the agent for this challenge.

Are you saying that when you click "Go to RBSC Portal" you get the 500 error? What landscape are you on? I'm not really an expert on RBSC, which is not specific to SAP Build Process Automation. It is used to deliver software and check licenses and export controls and perhaps other details. It took me a while to figure out what I needed to do, which I think was more complicated because I am at SAP. For you, you should just need an S-user and a technical user (which I think you create after having an S-user).

https://help.sap.com/docs/RBSC/0a64be17478d4f5ba45d14ab62b0d74c/175673b12feb41739df4f041db52fe76.html?locale=en-US

More info on RBSC in relation to SAP Build Process Automation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/install-desktop-agent-3-and-manage-updates

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: @jexpo I think i was too easy on you guys 💪

entry.title: Re: 🇫🇷 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Paris, France)
entry.content: Updated to Tuesday ... thanks for pointing out

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: @vishalakshmi Glad you escaped, but also glad it was a challenge 😺

entry.title: Re: How to Call OData Functions from SAP Build Apps
entry.content: @Lamiafarhat Sorry for the delayed response because of Passover holiday. Can you explain some more what is your situation?

entry.title: Now Define Variables from Pre-existing Schemas
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps now has the ability to create page variables based on existing schemas, like from other variables, data entities, and most interestingly, processes. This is really helpful.

I especially like that for every process type of schema, you can get both the input and output schemas -- more on this below.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT: I'm sure this feature was an idea for a while, but I first heard about when the new Processes flow functions were released a year ago. It was great that you could now trigger processes from SAP Build Apps without all the manual calling of an API, but you still had to create the variables for the inputs and outputs -- if you made one data type mistake the process would fail. So the answer was for the schemas to be easily inherited from the process, and now that is possible.

The Basics

When creating a new page variable, you can still define it from scratch, but now you can simply copy the schema from somewhere else:

Important to note that these definitions copy the schema onto the new variable. You can then modify the variable definition -- there is "linking".

  • Variables: There are many times I need multiple copies of the same data -- I want to modify the data but keep the original in a different variable.
  • Actions and Process: With SAP Build Apps now able to easily call actions and processes, it is helpful to not have to create your own variables to hold the returned data, something you had to do up until now.
  • Entities: This you could do with Data Variables, but there were times when you wanted to modify that data without disturbing the original data.

Use with Processes

I got to understand the feature because I recently updated tutorials for integrating SAP Build Apps with SAP Build Process Automation, and the ability to quickly create variables made the tutorial easier to understand and quicker to perform.

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

Triggering a Process

When triggering a process, all I had to do was create a page variable from the process schema, indicating:

  1. Base it on a process
  2. Use my process
  3. Use the schemas involved with the APIs for triggering a process
  4. Use the inputs of the process

One of the pains before was that essentially in SAP Build Apps, when defining the REST APIs for calling SAP Build Process Automations, you could only have one schema definition, either the input or the output. Now, I can create page variables for the inputs and for the outputs.

Here, I set up the page variable for the inputs of our process inputs, which is quite complicated.

To trigger the process, all I had to do was bind the page variable to the fields. Then, when I click the button to trigger, I drag a Trigger Process flow function, and set the Input Parameters to the context property of the page variable. No more setting object properties and each field separately.

I can still change the binding type to Object Properties and set the fields manually, or I can use a Formula to make modifications to the page variable. 

Retrieving Instances

Getting info about the process instances was even simpler, and the ability to create both input and output schemas helped.

This time I created a variable based on the Retrieve Process Instances schemas, and used the output schema.

This provided the standard fields that are part of every such API call.

Now all I had to do was bind the page variable to the UI. And in the logic, I just put the output of the Retrieve Process Instances flow function into the variable. Really easy.

It took really no time to be able to view the list of process instances.

Tutorials

Again, you can see the whole SAP Build Apps integration with SAP Build Process Automation in the revised sales order tutorials, which are now even faster to do, less than an hour to do both the process and the app. 

https://developers.sap.com/mission.build-workshop-sales-order.html

entry.title: Format and Display JSON in Your App
entry.content:
I wouldn't have thought this would be a big deal, but I recently got a call from a colleague in Ireland who was building an app for an important customer. And the customer had a requirement to retrieve data and display it in formatted JSON.

So my colleague asked: How do you you display formatted JSON?

At first, we both thought you could simply use the ENCODE_JSON formula function and then display in a Text field, but that did not work. But then I discovered (with the help of a friend 😽) that that function has a parameter, and that is all you need.

How to Do It

To demonstrate, I connected to the Northwind Product entity.

I then created a data variable that returned the first product, again, just to have some data.

On my UI, I created a container with a text box, whose content was set to a page variable, textToDisplay (with an initial value). I used the built-in style Primary Card to make it look nice.

SIDE NOTE: I used the custom width/height setting to make the container a minimum size but let it expand with the data. 

I then added a Set Page Variable in the data variable logic to set the page variable.

And I set the page variable to the formula below. Note some interesting things:

  • If you set the Spaces parameter of ENCODE_JSON, you will get the JSON nicely formatted.
  • Again, notice the formula editor has really good documentation on every function (if I would only read it 🤦).
ENCODE_JSON(data.Products1,4)

The result?

Without the Spaces parameter, it would have displayed like this:

Making It Editable

You could also put the text in an input box so a user could edit it.

Then you could provide a button to validate the JSON they type by using a simple JavaScript flow function.

I set the input to the page variable, changed the output to true/false, and used the following code:

If I click Validate with the default JSON:

If I make an error:

👉 Let me know if this was helpful, and maybe how you would improve on this.

entry.title: Re: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event...
entry.content:
These are tutorials designed to be done in our brand new Event-Based Processes CodeJams, whose world premiere will be in Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland, in a few weeks.

Advanced Event Mesh is a paid service not yet available in SAP BTP trial accounts. If you are interested in this set of tutorials, please consider either coming to our 2 planned CodeJams on this topic, or organize one in your area (costs are minimal for the host, free for participants) and we'd be happy to run an event.

entry.title: Re: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event...
entry.content:
The system we have provisioned is only for our events. I hope we can create a trial that can support this content. It's worth looking at @ajmaradiaga 's videos on setting S/4 to create events and Process Automation to receive and disperse the events.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6hb9l0ss5ec

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QfxI7ufCA24&t=2s

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CnPUhnKcl-k

entry.title: Re: How to pass filters in SAP BTP destination REST API integration in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
@PratikShekokar11 Can you explain more what you are looking for and what trouble you are having?

P.S.: This question and answer is a little out of date since you can now use actions in SAP Build Apps, and in the action you can define whatever you want for the $filter property, including using placeholders and passing those when you call the action from with your app.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: @S4_mir There was really no rush ... but I'm glad you made it 😊

entry.title: Add Value Help with SAP Build Actions (free project)
entry.content:
Value help is one of the key features that can make a UI really friendly. Here's how to do it without much work, using the new integration of Actions with SAP Build Apps.

Even if you do not need value help, you can learn from this project:

  • Actions (including custom $filter)
  • 🆕 New page variables based on existing schema feature
  • Custom events
  • Popups (including showing/hiding and absolute positioning)
  • Layouting and positioning 
  • Events related to input box
  • 🆕 Integrating actions into SAP Build Apps 
  • Working with destinations to retrieve S/4HANA data
  • Paging and filtering

As always we will use the A_BusinessPartner entity so the user can easily select a business partner. The app has 2 ways to provide the value help:

🟪From within an Input Box:

🟪From another screen that lists all the business partners, using paging and filtering:

NOTE: This technique is good for an OData v2 service. You can do the same for a v4 service, though there you could use the CONTAINS keyword in the action or even skip the action and use a "Get record collection" to do the filtering work, since for v4 services this flow function provides the contains filtering option.

Much of the idea for this UI is based on a blog by @botazhat and her blog How to add a search bar with suggestions in SAP Build Apps. I have enhanced it by using actions, and making it work with v2 services and taking data directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

Create Action for Getting Help Values

I think it is better design to use an action to get the help values since it.

  • Hides the API calls from the app developer
  • Hides the filtering syntax, which can be complex and different between OData v2 and v4
  • Makes the front-end development simpler

You can download the project, though it is quite simple to create yourself.

All you have to do to get started is create an action based on the BusinessPartner API service, and use the simple GET all business partners call from the A_BusinessPartner entity.

You will make a few small changes to make it easier to work with:

  • Add filterString as an input parameter to get a string to search in the business partner name.
  • Set defaults for some of the OData query parameters:
    • Set $top to 20, to retrieve only 20 matches at once (this can be overridden by the app)
    • Set $orderby to sort by BusinessPartnerFullName is ascending order (but you can change this)
    • Set $inlinecount to allpages, to return the total number of records, for the case where we paginate through the values.

For the filtering, we set the $filter input. Click the row, which opens the side panel. Then in the side panel, click to open the expression editor.

This will let you you set the $filter clause, either with a GUI or manually by typing in whatever you want.

This is the expression I used, to search for all matches on the BusinessPartnerFullName field using the value in filterString, and eliminating all business partners with no name. Note in v4 we would have had simpler syntax with the CONTAINS keyword.

substringof('${filterString}',BusinessPartnerFullName) eq true and BusinessPartnerFullName ne ''

Release and publish the action.

 

The Project

You can download the project we used to see how the UI, variables and logic. To use it, you'll have to connect your own deployed action. I've tried to make it as generic as possible.

There 2 pages, each providing a different way to get value help:

  • Home Page: Contains the form for business partner ID and name, plus the dropdown to search for a specific business partner.
  • Business Partners: Contains a list of all business partners that you can paginate through or search through.

And we have 2 app variables to store the business partner ID and name.

UI for Dropdown Help

For both pages, we create an integration to the action, and to the destination to be used with the action. Notice that here we create an integration to a destination without enabling any entities -- this was a little weird to me the first time I saw it. 

For variables, we have these:

  • Action_In and Action_Out are created automatically with the new feature that allows you to create variables from an action's schema.
  • filterString is the search string.
  • events is a way to control the updates when someone types fast and reduce the number of calls to the backend (write a comment if you want to learn more). 

And the logic is pretty simple with only 3 main flow functions (skipping all the flow functions related to the events variable):

  1. We capture when someone types in the search field (EVENT)
  2. We ignore when the search string is less than 3 characters (IF CONDITION)
  3. We then run the action (TRIGGER ACTION)
  4. We set the variable to hold the returned values (SET PAGE VARIABLE)

We also added some simple logic to handle when the user leaves the search box or presses escape (clear the value help) or when the user returns (run the original logic above with custom event). 

As for the UI, the main thing is the container holding the value help is absolutely positioned so it aligns with the input box:

And its visibility is set only when there are options to display:

UI for Selecting from All Values

Instead of using a dropdown, we added an icon at the end of the input box that navigates to a different page for selecting the business partner. 

This takes us to a separate page where we display all the business partners, and we can use filtering or paging to find the record we want.

This is the only logic we have, on the page logic canvas (except for clicking one of the business partners and returning to the home page).

When we come to the page we load all the business partners, and we have a custom event to refresh the page whenever we need to.

Then we have 3 flows when different page variables change:

  • User changes search string: We reset the page number to 1 and the OData $skip parameter to 0, and call the backend again.
  • User navigates to a different page: We recalculate the OData $skip parameter, and call the backend again.
  • System changes the # of retrieved records: We recalculate the number of pages we have. This occurs basically whenever we call the backend.

 

Room for Improvement

There are small things we could have done to make it even better:

  • Put the icon for the full business partner list inside the input box, making it a custom component (something Bota did in her blog)
  • Add multi-selection and wildcard characters
  • Package it to make it more generic for any data entity

Also, I wanted to eliminate flashing when the user enters a search string, and there was a tradeoff between the flashing and the speed. I might change my calculus to make it faster.

I'll work on these. In the meantime, tell me what you thought of these techniques, including how you would improve them.

😸

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content: That's great 😸 I see you have registered on this page. In about a week, we will request confirmation and send out additional information about the event. But you are now registered, and looking forward to seeing you there.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: @vishalakshmi I believe they are awarded as a batch a week or so after the end of the month when the challenge was introduced. I will check on that this week.

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇭 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (Risch-Rotkreuz, Switzerland)
entry.content: I will check on Sunday … but you might want to simply try to run the setup again, or to create a trial in US (Va) … and sometimes there simply is a problem with installation that affects everyone

entry.title: Re: Retention period for tasks completed in Build Process Automation
entry.content: Which data are you looking for? I can see process instances from at least 2 years ago, and I do not think we changed any settings. What system are you working on?

entry.title: Re: need help connecting Salesforce to SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
And what happens at runtime?

  • Use alert messages to display the error or other info that would help diagnose.
  • When running the call, open the developer tools and make sure the request -- URL and payload -- is being sent as you expected.

entry.title: Re: Retention period for tasks completed in Build Process Automation
entry.content: And the filter is set to show Completed instances? By default, Completed instances are not shown. Perhaps there is a setting that removes them from the list but I am not aware of that.
